title: eleusine coracana subsp. africana
intro: Eleusine coracana subsp. africana, also known as finger millet, is a subspecies of the eleusine coracana plant, a type of small-grained cereal crop native to Africa. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of eleusine coracana subsp. africana include reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving digestive health, and providing essential nutrients such as fiber, iron, and calcium. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, flour, or supplement, and its leaves and seeds can also be used to make a medicinal infusion.
